display.purpose: Page-level titles that appear once per screen (e.g. the login card title).

headline.purpose: Section titles, e.g. the exams-list page header.

title.purpose: Panel/card headers nested within a page, e.g. the exam-form overlay title.

body.purpose: Table cells, form inputs and labels, all default running text.

label.purpose: Table column headers, status badges, small metadata tags.

shadows.name: resting-card
shadows.purpose: Default at-rest depth for every card, panel, and table surface.

shadows.name: raised-hover
shadows.purpose: Row hover in dense tables; momentary focus/drag state on cards.

shadows.name: overlay
shadows.purpose: The exams-list form-panel and any future modal — the single floating elevation step.

motion.name: ease-out-expo
motion.purpose: Entrances: form-panel opening, row insertion after create.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one action per screen the admin should take next (نشر / حفظ / إنشاء).
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #1a56db; color: #ffffff; font-family: Cairo, "Segoe UI", Tahoma, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; padding: 10px 20px; border: none; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), box-shadow 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #153f9e; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #ffffff, 0 0 0 4px #e8eefc, 0 0 0 5px #1a56db; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { background: #e5e7eb; color: #6b7280; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: A supporting action next to a primary button (إغلاق / إلغاء).
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #ffffff; color: #1a56db; font-family: Cairo, "Segoe UI", Tahoma, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; padding: 9px 19px; border: 1px solid #1a56db; border-radius: 6px; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #e8eefc; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #ffffff, 0 0 0 4px #e8eefc, 0 0 0 5px #1a56db; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Default single-line field used across every form (login, question-form, exam-form).
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; font-family: Cairo, "Segoe UI", Tahoma, Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-label { font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.02em; color: #6b7280; } .ds-input { background: #ffffff; color: #111827; font-size: 0.875rem; padding: 8px 12px; border: 1px solid #e5e7eb; border-radius: 6px; transition: border-color 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), box-shadow 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #6b7280; } .ds-input:focus { outline: none; border-color: #1a56db; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px #e8eefc; } .ds-input.ds-input-error { border-color: #ef4444; }

components.name: Card Surface
components.description: The lifted-surface treatment for panels, the login card, and the exam-form overlay — depth from shadow only, never a border.
components.html:
تعديل امتحان

تفاصيل الامتحان تظهر هنا.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #ffffff; border-radius: 6px; padding: 16px; box-shadow: 0 2px 12px 0 rgba(17, 24, 39, 0.08); font-family: Cairo, "Segoe UI", Tahoma, Arial, sans-serif; transition: box-shadow 0.22s cubic-bezier(0.16,1,0.3,1); } .ds-card:hover { box-shadow: 0 6px 20px 0 rgba(17, 24, 39, 0.12); } .ds-card-title { margin: 0 0 8px; font-size: 1.125rem; font-weight: 600; color: #111827; } .ds-card-body { margin: 0; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.6; color: #6b7280; }

components.name: Data Table Row
components.description: The signature list pattern (question bank, topics, exams): flat header, hairline row dividers, tinted hover — no card chrome.
components.css: .ds-table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; font-family: Cairo, "Segoe UI", Tahoma, Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; } .ds-table thead { background: #f9fafb; } .ds-table th { text-align: right; padding: 8px 16px; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: 0.02em; color: #6b7280; border-bottom: 1px solid #e5e7eb; } .ds-table td { padding: 12px 16px; color: #111827; border-bottom: 1px solid #e5e7eb; } .ds-row { background: #ffffff; transition: background-color 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-row:hover { background: #e8eefc; } .ds-btn-primary { background: #1a56db; color: #fff; border: none; border-radius: 6px; padding: 6px 14px; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 600; cursor: pointer; }

components.name: Nav Item
components.description: Admin-layout header link — flat, hairline rule beneath the whole header, Authority Blue underline marks the active route.
components.html: الامتحاناتالأسئلة
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; gap: 24px; padding: 0 0 1px; border-bottom: 1px solid #e5e7eb; font-family: Cairo, "Segoe UI", Tahoma, Arial, sans-serif; } .ds-nav-link { padding: 12px 4px; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 400; color: #6b7280; text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 2px solid transparent; transition: color 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), border-color 0.15s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-nav-link:hover { color: #111827; } .ds-nav-link-active { color: #1a56db; font-weight: 600; border-bottom-color: #1a56db; }

narrative.overview:
This is the working console behind a Ministry of Justice exam system: an internal tool for admins who configure exams, curate question banks, and publish with confidence that the numbers add up. The Official Ledger treats every screen like a page in a well-kept register — dense with real data, laid out so the eye finds what it needs in one pass, and quietly authoritative rather than decorated. It is modern and professional, never sterile: motion and feedback are present and controlled, not suppressed in the name of looking "official," and not performed in the name of looking "modern."

The system explicitly rejects the loud, colorful commercial-SaaS marketing look (gradient-as-decoration, hero-metric tiles, kicker/eyebrow labels, generic AI-tool scaffolding) and it equally rejects the opposite failure mode — a purely static, robotic government form with no feedback at all. Every action an admin takes (publish, close, archive, delete, save) earns a clear, immediate, motion-backed acknowledgment. Arabic RTL is the native language of this interface, not a mirrored afterthought: type, iconography, and motion direction are built RTL-first.
keyCharacteristics: Dense, scannable data tables and matrices over decorative cards
One accent color (Authority Blue), used deliberately and sparingly
Calm, breathable component spacing even in data-dense screens
Layered elevation: surfaces read their depth from a persistent, soft shadow, not from borders
RTL-native layout, motion, and iconography throughout

rules.name: The One Ledger Rule
rules.body: Authority Blue appears on at most one primary action per view and on active/selected state — never as a decorative background wash, section divider, or illustrative color. Its rarity is what makes it read as "the thing to do here."

rules.name: The One-Family Rule
rules.body: Every weight and size on screen comes from Cairo. If a second family shows up, it's a bug, not a design choice.

rules.name: The No-Ghost-Card Rule
rules.body: A surface gets a border or a shadow, never both. Cards and panels use the Shadow Vocabulary with no border; only true dividers (a table's header rule, a form's field separator) use Hairline Grey, and those never carry a shadow.

dos: Do use Cairo as the only type family across the app; vary weight and size, never introduce a second family.
Do give every card, panel, and modal-style overlay the Resting-card shadow (0 2px 12px 0 rgba(17,24,39,0.08)) at rest — depth is always visible, not just on hover.
Do confirm every state-changing action (publish / close / archive / delete / save) with an immediate, motion-backed acknowledgment — a toast, an inline status change, or both.
Do build navigation, tables, and motion RTL-first: reading order, icon direction, and animation direction all flow right-to-left as the native case, not as a mirrored LTR default.
Do keep Authority Blue to primary actions, active/selected state, and focus rings only.
donts: Don't combine a border and a shadow on the same surface — pick the shadow.

Don't reach for a loud, colorful commercial-SaaS marketing aesthetic: no gradient-as-decoration, no gradient text, no hero-metric tiles, no tiny uppercase eyebrow labels above sections, no generic AI-tool scaffolding.
Don't ship a purely static, feedback-free "government form" screen — motion on interaction is part of the brand, not optional polish.
Don't let PrimeNG's own default theme values (its --primary-color: #3B82F6 and "Inter var" font, both currently unused anywhere in the codebase) leak into a new component by accident.
Don't over-design for personality's sake — when trust/legibility and delight conflict, trust and legibility win.
